raw:
{
  "judge_id": "autogen",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 4,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "R1.7": 4,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 4,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "Identifies several unsupported claims and provides detailed critiques, but does not audit every claim against the table/figure.",
    "R1.4": "Names one prior work (Vaibhava and Hermansky) and articulates the delta in terms of a fabricated citation, but lacks broader context or comparison with other works.",
    "R1.7": "Flags several missing citations and provides bibliographic precision for some references, but does not audit every load-bearing citation.",
    "RX.1": "Discloses uncertainty about the Vaibhava and Hermansky citation being fabricated, but does not mark confidence in all recommendations.",
    "RX.3": "Audits major claims against prior work and identifies missing citations with bibliographic precision.",
    "RX.4": "Identifies harmful content (fabricated citation) and minor regressions (unclear baseline for WER reduction), but does not introduce new errors or fabrications.",
    "RX.5": "Traces major rewrites by providing detailed critiques, but does not annotate every non-cosmetic change."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.85,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.95,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: Identifies several unsupported claims and provides detailed critiques, but does not audit every claim against the table/figure.
rationale.R1.4: Names one prior work (Vaibhava and Hermansky) and articulates the delta in terms of a fabricated citation, but lacks broader context or comparison with other works.
rationale.R1.7: Flags several missing citations and provides bibliographic precision for some references, but does not audit every load-bearing citation.
rationale.RX.1: Discloses uncertainty about the Vaibhava and Hermansky citation being fabricated, but does not mark confidence in all recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: Audits major claims against prior work and identifies missing citations with bibliographic precision.
rationale.RX.4: Identifies harmful content (fabricated citation) and minor regressions (unclear baseline for WER reduction), but does not introduce new errors or fabrications.
rationale.RX.5: Traces major rewrites by providing detailed critiques, but does not annotate every non-cosmetic change.
